decisions.briefing: Markets posted a mild down day — NASDAQ off 0.7%, S&P flat — with no compelling catalyst to act. Cash sits at $444.94, just below the $500 threshold required to trigger new position buys, so we hold all six current positions and wait for either a position to close or cash to accumulate before entering new names. Top candidates like LEGN, EMAT, and SPHR remain on radar for the next entry opportunity.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities drifted lower Tuesday as tech stocks led the pullback, with the NASDAQ falling 0.7% amid lingering concerns about stretched valuations in the AI and semiconductor sectors following a strong April rally. The S&P 500 held up better, losing only 0.2%, as defensive sectors like healthcare and consumer staples provided partial offsets. Traders remain cautious ahead of upcoming inflation data, keeping risk appetite measured across the board.
market_education.summary_zh: 美股週二小幅走低，科技股領跌，納斯達克指數下跌0.7%，原因是投資者擔憂人工智能及半導體板塊在四月強勁反彈後估值過高。標普500指數相對抗跌，僅下滑0.2%，醫療保健及必需消費品等防禦性板塊提供了一定支撐。市場在等待即將公佈的通脹數據之際，整體風險偏好維持謹慎，操作節奏趨於保守。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: A market pullback is a short-term decline in prices — typically 3–10% — within a longer uptrend. Unlike a full correction or bear market, pullbacks are considered healthy pauses that allow valuations to reset before the next move higher. Savvy investors often use these dips to enter high-conviction positions at better prices rather than chasing momentum at peaks.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 市場回調是指在較長期上升趨勢中出現的短暫價格下跌，通常幅度在3%至10%之間。與全面調整或熊市不同，回調被視為一種健康的休整，讓估值在下一波上漲前有機會重置。有經驗的投資者往往把握這類回調時機，以更合理的價格進入高信心度的標的，而非在高位追漲。
